target: Percolation Threshold Theory x T Cell Infiltration in Solid Tumors

pipeline_error.source: Alexander-Orbach conjecture (proven only for d >= 19, Kozma & Nachmias 2009)
pipeline_error.impact: MSD exponent at p_c changes from 0.53 to 0.695 -- affects H1, H2, H3, H6
pipeline_error.severity: SIGNIFICANT (30% error in key predicted observable)

hypotheses.title: LOX Crosslink Density as Bond Occupation Probability Predicts a Universal Immune Exclusion Threshold at p ~ 0.21-0.25

LOX crosslink density to bond occupation probability mapping entirely PARAMETRIC
Fraction of immune exclusion variance explained by ECM topology not quantified
Directionality notation requires careful reading despite self-critique correction
strengths: Genuinely novel application -- zero prior art for percolation + T cell infiltration
All major citations verified (Wang 2013, Nicolas-Boluda 2021, Zeitz 2017, Saha 2024)
Mechanism chain LOX -> collagen -> pore constriction -> T cell exclusion grounded in 2024 literature
Quantitative falsifiable predictions with achievable experiments
Properly acknowledges active particle correction and Ashworth 2015 prior art

percolation threshold collagen crosslink density immune cell migration 2024 2025
factual_errors: d_w = 3.8 should be 2.878; MSD exponent 0.53 should be 0.695

Lattice constant ambiguity (2 um vs 10 um) shifts all xi values by 5x

Power-law cluster distributions are non-unique (branching processes, preferential attachment)
MSD exponent error inherited from H1 (0.53 should be ~0.69)
strengths: Parameter-free prediction from universality class
Testable on EXISTING spatial transcriptomics datasets
Specific exponent distinguishable from random distributions
Fills genuine gap in hot/cold tumor boundary quantification

Lattice constant choice is arbitrary, affecting all numerical predictions

Testable on existing clinical cohorts

hypotheses.title: Collagen I/III Ratio Acts as a Lattice Topology Switch Between Two Universality Classes
weaknesses: p_c values for anisotropic collagen lattices (0.35-0.45) are entirely unvalidated speculation
Macrophage depletion effects are multimodal -- impossible to isolate collagen topology contribution
Collagen I and III differ in biochemistry and mechanics, not just topology
p_c shift magnitude (~40-80%) likely overestimated for realistic anisotropy
Title says 'universality class switch' but text correctly says same class, different p_c -- misleading
strengths: Fusilier 2025 Science Immunology is strong verified biological foundation
Percolation prediction (same exponents, shifted p_c) is mathematically correct
Novel quantitative interpretation of Fusilier findings
Testable in principle with collagen type-controlled experiments

factual_errors: Title misleading -- should say 'shifted p_c within same universality class'

BAPN is not LOX-specific -- affects LOXL1-4 with different network effects
PMID 39101793 uses PXS-5505, not LOX-IN-3 as implied -- factual imprecision
Non-random crosslink distribution violates clean power-law scaling assumption
Alternative threshold mechanisms (pharmacokinetic, compensatory) produce different exponents
strengths: Specific exponent 0.417 distinguishes percolation from other dose-response models
Directly clinically relevant (therapeutic window optimization)
Technically feasible multi-dose experiment
No dose-response curve exists in literature -- filling clear gap

d_w = 3.8 incorrect (should be 2.878)
T cell motility poorly described by ABP model (amoeboid, chemotactic, contact-guided)
strengths: Creative counterintuitive prediction with testable consequences
Self-trapping phenomenon verified in physics (Zeitz 2017)
Would explain real clinical observation if correct
Honest self-critique acknowledges Pe limitation
3D active percolation confirmed as unexplored frontier (arXiv 2603.04602)

hypotheses.title: Universality Class Critical Exponents Are Tumor-Type-Invariant
hypotheses.kill_vector: Mechanism Kill + Logic Kill: correlated disorder (CAF proximity, fiber alignment, TGF-beta gradients) in real tumor ECM violates standard percolation universality class requirements. Weinrib 1984 extended Harris criterion shows correlations are relevant when a < 2/nu = 2.28. Tumor ECM correlations almost certainly satisfy this condition. The hypothesis is internally inconsistent -- claims universality while listing four mechanisms that would destroy it.
weaknesses: Correlated disorder from CAF proximity and fiber alignment changes universality class
Hypothesis internally inconsistent -- claims universality while listing four mechanisms that destroy it
Directed percolation may be relevant for aligned tumors (different exponents)
Practically untestable -- requires high-precision exponents in rare near-critical tumors
Strongest version of the theory is the most fragile
strengths: If true, would be the most powerful prediction in the set
Correctly identifies universality as potential feature
Honest self-critique lists relevant threats
novelty_searches: tumor ECM fiber alignment correlated disorder random lattice percolation universality class

hypotheses.title: TGF-beta Autocrine Signaling Constitutes Bond-Correlated Percolation with Non-Linear LOX/Anti-TGF-beta Synergy
weaknesses: TGF-beta activation is cell-contact-dependent via integrin alphaVbeta6, not free-diffusing -- effective range ~10-30 um, not 50-100 um
Direction of p_c shift with positive correlation is model-dependent
TGF-beta blockade has many effects beyond de-correlating crosslinks
Synergy prediction is qualitative, not quantitative -- no magnitude estimate
Prakash 1992 citation unverified
strengths: Creative integration of correlated percolation theory with tumor biology
Testable combination therapy prediction with clear experimental design
Fills gap in LOX + anti-TGF-beta synergy mechanism understanding
TGF-beta -> LOX -> collagen -> TGF-beta positive feedback loop is real biology
novelty_searches: TGF-beta latent activation integrin alpha-v-beta-6 local cell contact dependent
Weinrib 1984 correlated percolation Harris criterion exponents
citations_failed: Prakash 1992 (unverified -- topic exists but specific citation could not be confirmed)
factual_errors: TGF-beta diffusion range 50-100 um -- actual effective signaling range ~10-30 um due to contact-dependent activation

critic_questions.question: What is the correct walk dimension d_w for 3D percolation, and what MSD exponent does it predict? The pipeline used d_w = 3.8 from the Alexander-Orbach conjecture, but the measured value is d_w = 2.878, giving alpha = 0.695 not 0.53. All hypotheses quoting alpha = 0.53 must be corrected.

Can you provide a quantitative estimate of what fraction of immune exclusion variance is explained by ECM topology versus other mechanisms (chemokine gradients, immunosuppressive signaling, metabolic competition)?

How does TGF-beta contact-dependent activation (via integrin alpha-v-beta-6) change the spatial correlation structure assumed in H8? The hypothesis assumes free-diffusion signaling over 50-100 um, but activation is cell-contact-dependent with effective range ~10-30 um.

What is the actual measured Fisher exponent tau for 3D percolation? H2 uses tau = 1 + d/d_f = 2.19, but numerically measured value is tau ~ 2.31. Which value should be used for experimental predictions?

At what Peclet number does self-trapping become quantitatively significant for active particles in a random Lorentz gas? H6 claims Pe ~ 3 is sufficient, but Zeitz 2017 data suggest the effect is strongest at Pe >> 10.

meta_critique.kill_rate_assessment: Below 15% minimum. The low rate reflects a genuinely strong hypothesis set within a coherent framework. The one kill (H7) is well-justified by the Weinrib 1984 Harris criterion and internal inconsistency.
meta_critique.cross_hypothesis_redundancy: H1/H5 share core mechanism (LOX as p, threshold at p_c) but address different predictions. H2/H7 both address scaling but H7 killed does not invalidate H2.
meta_critique.systematic_error: d_w = 3.8 (should be 2.878) propagated from computational validator through 4 hypotheses. MSD exponent changes from 0.53 to 0.695.
